volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 4.9.743b - Section 5.2.810)

sections.label: Section 4.9.743b
sections.translation: By my faith , you scoundrel , I ’ ll give you cause to bewail your own self as well .

sections.label: Section 4.9.744b
sections.translation: I ’ m undone !

sections.label: Section 4.9.745
sections.translation: Come , walk before a magistrate , Procurer .

sections.label: Section 4.9.745b
sections.translation: Why do you summon me before a magistrate ?

sections.label: Section 4.9.746
sections.original: Illi apud praetorem dicam . sed ego in ius voco .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you there , before the Praetor . But before the magistrate I summon you .

sections.label: Section 4.9.747
sections.translation: Don ’ t you summon [*] a witness ?

sections.label: Section 4.9.747b
sections.translation: What , for your sake , hangdog , am I to be touching the ear of any being that ’ s free — you , who are here trading in persons , free citizens ?

sections.label: Section 4.9.750
sections.translation: Let me but speak —

sections.label: Section 4.9.750b
sections.translation: I won ’ t .

sections.label: Section 4.9.750c
sections.translation: Hear me .

sections.label: Section 4.9.750d
sections.translation: I ’ m deaf . Walk on — follow me this way ( dragging him .) you villanous mouser after maidens ! Follow after me this way , my daughter , to the Praetor .

sections.label: Section 4.9.752b
sections.translation: I ’ ll follow .

sections.label: Section 5.1.753
sections.original: Hostíbus victis , civíbus salvis , re plácida , pacibus pérfectis ,
sections.translation: The foe subdued , the citizens safe , the state in tranquillity , peace fully ratified , the war finished , our affairs prospering , the army and the garrisons untouched ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.755
sections.original: cum béne nos , Iuppiter , iúvisti , dique álii omnes caelípotentes ,
sections.translation: inasmuch , Jupiter , and all you other Deities potent in the heavens , you have kindly aided us , for that reason do I return and give you thanks ; because I have been fully revenged upon my foe . Now , for this reason , among my partners will I divide and allot the spoil .

sections.label: Section 5.1.758
sections.original: íte foras : híc volo ante óstium et iánuam
sections.translation: ( To the SLAVES in the house , who obey his orders .) Come out of doors ; here , before the entrance and the door , I wish to entertain my commates with hospitality . Lay down the couches here ; place here the things that are usual .

sections.label: Section 5.1.759a
sections.original: hic státui volo primum † aliqua mihi ,
sections.translation: Here am I determined that my eagle [*] shall be first pitched ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.760
sections.original: unde égo omnis hilaros , lúdentis , laetíficantis faciam út fiant ,
sections.translation: from which spot I ’ ll cause all to become merry , joyous , and delighted , by the aid of whom those things which I wished to be effected have been rendered for me easy to be done ; for worthless is the man who knows how to receive a kindness , and knows not how to return it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.763
sections.original: Toxíle mi , cur ego síne te sum , cur tu aútem sine me es ?
sections.translation: My Toxilus , why am I without you ? And why are you without me ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.763b
sections.translation: Come then , my own one , approach me , and embrace me , please .

sections.label: Section 5.1.764b
sections.translation: Indeed I will .

sections.label: Section 5.1.764c
sections.translation: O , nothing is there more sweet than this .

sections.label: Section 5.1.765
sections.original: sed , amábo , oculus meus , quín lectis nos áctutum comméndamus ?
sections.translation: But , there ’ s a dear , apple of my eye , why don ’ t we at once betake us to the couches ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.766
sections.original: Omnía quae tu vis , éa cupio .
sections.translation: Everything that you wish , the same do I desire .

sections.label: Section 5.1.766b
sections.original: Mutúa fiunt a me . age , age ergo ,
sections.translation: It ’ s mutual . Come , come-come then . You , Sagaristio , recline in the upper place .

sections.label: Section 5.1.767b
sections.original: Ego níl moror : cedo parem , quém pepigi .
sections.translation: I don ’ t at all care for it . Give ne but equal shares in what I ’ ve earned .

sections.label: Section 5.1.768
sections.translation: All in good time .

sections.label: Section 5.1.768b
sections.translation: For me that good time is too late .

sections.label: Section 5.1.768c
sections.original: Hoc age , accúmbe . hunc diém suavem
sections.translation: Attend to the matter in hand . Take your place ; this delightful day let ’ s keep as a joyous birthday of mine . ( To the SLAVES .) Bring water for our hands ; arrange the table .

sections.label: Section 5.1.770
sections.original: do hanc tíbi florentem flórenti . tu hic éris dictatrix nóbis .
sections.translation: ( The SLAVES obey , and the GUESTS take their places .) To you , blooming one ( addressing LEMNISELENE .) I give this blooming wreath . ( Places a garland on her head .) You shall be our governess here .

sections.label: Section 5.1.771
sections.original: Age , púere , ab summo séptenis cyathís committe hos lúdos .
sections.translation: Come , lad , commence these games from the top with a round of seven cups .

sections.label: Section 5.1.772
sections.original: Move mánus , propera , Paegniúm , tarde cyathós mihi das , cedo sáne .
sections.translation: Bestir your hands ; make haste . Paegnium , you are slow in giving me the cups ; really , do give them . Here ’ s luck to me , luck to you , luck to my mistress , luck to us all . This much wished-for day has been sent me by the Gods this day , inasmuch as I am allowed to embrace you a free woman .

sections.label: Section 5.1.774b
sections.translation: By your own agency it was effected .

sections.label: Section 5.1.775
sections.original: Bene omníbus nobis . hoc méa manus tuaé poclúm donat , ut amántem
sections.translation: ( Giving him the cup .) This cup my hand presents to you , as it becomes a mistress to her love .

sections.label: Section 5.1.776b
sections.translation: Give it me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.776c
sections.translation: Take it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.776d
sections.original: Bene eí qui invidét mi et eí qui hoc gaúdet .
sections.translation: Here ’ s luck to him who envies me , and to him who rejoices in this joy .

sections.label: Section 5.2.777
sections.original: Qui súnt † qui erunt quiqué fuerunt quiqué futuri sunt pósthac ,
sections.translation: Those who are , and those who shall be , and those who have been , and those who are to be hereafter , all of them I singly by far surpass , in being the most wretched of men alive .

sections.label: Section 5.2.779_780
sections.original: perii , ínterii . pessímus hic mi dies hódie inluxit córruptor ,
sections.translation: I ’ m undone , ruined quite ! This day has shone upon me the most unfortunate of days ; that ruiner Toxilus has so outmanœuvred me , and has so laid waste my property ! A whole cartload of silver , to my misfortune , have I upset , and lost , and have not that for which I did upset it . May all the Deities utterly confound that Persian , and all Persians , and all persons besides ! in such a way has Toxilus , the wretch , conjured this up against me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.785
sections.original: quia eí fidem non habui árgenti , eo míhi eas machinas mólitust ;
sections.translation: Because I didn ’ t trust him for the money , for that reason has he contrived this plan against me ,— a fellow , that , by my faith , if I only live , I ’ ll bring to torture and the fetters ; if , indeed , his master ever returns here , as I trust he will . ( Catching sight of the REVELLERS .) But what is it I see ? Do look at that . What play is this ? By my troth , they ’ re carousing here surely . I ’ ll accost them . ( He goes up to them .) O worthy sir ( to TOXILUS .) my greetings to you — you , too ( to LEMNISELENE .) my worthy freed-woman .

sections.label: Section 5.2.789_790b
sections.translation: Why surely this is Dordalus .

sections.label: Section 5.2.789_790c
sections.translation: Invite him , then , to come .

sections.label: Section 5.2.791
sections.translation: Come here , if you like .

sections.label: Section 5.2.791b
sections.translation: ( Aside .) Come , let ’ s sing his praises .

sections.label: Section 5.2.791c
sections.translation: ( Aloud .) Dordalus , most delightful fellow , welcome , here ’ s a place for you ; take your place here ( pointing to a couch .) Bring water for his feet ( to the SLAVES .) Are you going to give it , lad ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.793
sections.original: Ne sís me uno digito áttigeris , ne te ád terram , scelus , ádfligam .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you , please , be touching me with a single finger , lest I should fell you to the ground , you villain .

sections.label: Section 5.2.794
sections.original: At tíbi ego hoc continuó cyatho oculum éxcutiam † tuum .
sections.translation: And I this very instant will be striking out your eye with this tankard .

sections.label: Section 5.2.795
sections.original: Quid aís , crux , stimulorúm tritor ? quo módo me hodie versávisti ,
sections.translation: What do you say , gallows [*] , you wearer-away of the whip ? How have you imposed upon me [*] to-day ? Into what embarrassments have you thrown me ? How have I been baulked about the Persian ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.797
sections.translation: You ’ ll be off with your abuse from here , if you are wise .

sections.label: Section 5.2.798
sections.original: At , bóna liberta , haec scívisti et me célavisti ?
sections.translation: But , my worthy freed-woman , you knew of this , and concealed it from me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.798b
sections.translation: It ’ s folly

sections.label: Section 5.2.799_800
sections.original: cui béne esse licet , eum praévorti litíbus . posterius ístaec te
sections.translation: for a person who can enjoy himself to turn to brawling in preference . ’ Twere more proper for you to arrange about those matters another time .

sections.label: Section 5.2.801b
sections.translation: My heart ’ s in flames .

sections.label: Section 5.2.801c
sections.original: Da illí cantharum , extingue ígnem , si
sections.translation: Give him a goblet , then ; put out the fire , if his heart ’ s in flames , that his head mayn ’ t be burnt .

sections.label: Section 5.2.803
sections.translation: You ’ re making sport of me , I find .

sections.label: Section 5.2.804
sections.original: Vín cinaedúm novom tíbi dari , Paégnium ?
sections.translation: Would you like [*] a new playfellow for you , Paegnium ? ( Pointing at DORDALUS .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.805
sections.original: quín elude , út soles , quándo libér locust hic .
sections.translation: But sport on as you are wont , as this is a place of freedom . ( PAEGNIUM struts about round DORDALUS .) O rare ! you do stalk in a princely style and right merrily .

sections.label: Section 5.2.807
sections.original: Decét me facétum esse , et húnc inridére
sections.translation: It befits me to be merry , and I ’ ve a longing to play this Procurer some pranks , since he ’ s deserving of it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.809
sections.translation: As you commenced , proceed .

sections.label: Section 5.2.809b
sections.translation: Take that , Procurer !

sections.label: Section 5.2.810
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ! he has almost knocked me down !
